campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell's city council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they honored K9 Lucas, a police dog who recently passed away. The council also approved updates to beekeeping rules, discussed a state bill about development near transit areas (with some council members sitting out due to conflicts of interest), and reviewed a study about creating homeless services in the West Valley region.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial — police department's first working dog
Beekeeping ordinance updated and adopted
Senate Bill 79 — transit-oriented development debate (3 council members recused)
West Valley Homeless Services feasibility study and regional task force proposal

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a special meeting to review and approve the yearly plans for five different city commissions that handle things like parks, libraries, historic preservation, and traffic safety. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year. The council also met privately to discuss a legal case the city may need to pursue.
keyTopics: Heritage Preservation Commission priorities for protecting historic sites
Parks and Recreation plans for upcoming community facilities and programs
Library and Public Art Commission goals for the library and public art installations
Traffic Safety Commission work to improve road safety around Saratoga
Planning Commission agenda for land use and development decisions

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met on March 24, 2026 to handle routine business including approving meeting minutes and financial reports. The council also adopted policies on weed control completion, housing affordability exemptions, and approved raising two new flags (Progress Pride and Juneteenth) at city facilities.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion for 2026
New rules for affordable housing unit transfers
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flags approved for city buildings
Updates on housing element implementation and transportation planning

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026, to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and check on progress with housing and general planning goals. The council also approved a new residential development project (Solana) and finalized plans for community health and senior services in 2026.
keyTopics: Approved the Solana Project residential development (Tract 10657)
Reviewed January 2026 city finances and investments
Confirmed progress on General Plan and Housing Element goals
Finalized 2026 Community Health and Senior Services work plan
Updated meeting minute procedures and formats

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to organize and finalize their upcoming agendas for April 7 and April 14, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to each agenda, set specific times for certain discussions if needed, and checked documents to make sure proper legal notices were given to the public.
keyTopics: Finalized the April 7, 2026 meeting agenda
Prepared the April 14, 2026 draft agenda
Scheduled specific times for agenda items to keep meetings on track
Reviewed public notice requirements for agenda items and documents
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, to approve previous meeting minutes and hear public comments. The council reviewed reports from various committees and discussed upcoming meetings for city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period for residents
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Contact Recreation Division at 650-903-6331 for agenda questions

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps fund affordable housing and community development projects. The commission also approved meeting minutes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking funding.
keyTopics: Federal grant funding for housing and community programs
Review of applications from organizations seeking CDBG money
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and thrive. They discussed programs to help shops get their licenses in order, ways to encourage people to shop locally through summer events, and financial incentives to attract new retail stores and startups to the city.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses get compliant without penalties
Summer Shop Local Campaign - promoting local businesses through city events
Economic Incentives for Retailers and Startups - attracting new businesses to Cupertino
Shopping Center Outreach - direct engagement with local malls and shopping areas
Public-Private Partnerships - using sponsorships to fund city events

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed matters related to historic buildings and landmarks in the community. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom to watch and participate in the discussion.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom participation)
Public comment submission deadline (12 p.m. day of meeting via email)
Historic preservation and landmark designation matters

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative housekeeping items like approving meeting minutes and planning the calendar for upcoming sessions. The council also approved Mayor Montano's travel to a 2026 wellness conference and confirmed a school district representative appointment to an economic committee.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling for future council meetings
Mayor Montano authorized to attend WELL 2026 Annual conference
New appointment of school district representative to Economic Committee
Administrative items managed by City Clerk Suzanne Guzzetta

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine items including officially recognizing March as Women's History Month. They approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and backup services (Mimecast) at about $93,000 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
